recommendations.title: Protect the highest-authority pillar
recommendations.detail: Coding for Kids: The Ultimate Guide for Parents (2026) has the strongest internal footprint. Use it as the model for cluster linking and conversion routing.

recommendations.title: Build links into underlinked pillars
recommendations.detail: 18 inferred pillars need more supporting links. Start with the first 8 contextual opportunities.

recommendations.title: Turn recent posts into supporting assets
recommendations.detail: New 2026 posts should link back to the correct evergreen guides, especially AI, Minecraft, Roblox, and coding-class pages.

recommendations.title: Clean up orphaned content
recommendations.detail: 13 posts have weak link signals. Decide whether to refresh, merge, redirect, or add them to a cluster.

confirmedPillars.title: Coding for Kids: The Ultimate Guide for Parents
confirmedPillars.notes: Confirmed by CodaKid team as a pillar blog to track.

technicalAudit.Impact: 34 pages caught in infinite redirect loops. Users and crawlers stuck. Wastes crawl budget.
technicalAudit.Fix Instructions: Find the URL with Cyrillic 'с' (not Latin 'c') in 'сodakid'. Set up a proper 301 redirect from the Cyrillic URL to the correct Latin version. Update all internal links pointing to this URL. URL contains: /unlocking-creativity-and-learning-with-сodakid-the-35-best-scratch-games-for-kids/

technicalAudit.Impact: Dead links throughout the site. Lost link equity, bad user experience, wasted crawl budget.
technicalAudit.Fix Instructions: Set up 301 redirects from each deleted URL to the most relevant existing page. Priority redirects: /coding-for-kids-the-ultimate-guide-for-parents-part-1/ → /coding-for-kids/, /private-tutoring-plans/ → /private-online-coding-classes/

technicalAudit.Impact: Homepage has no H1. Google uses H1 to understand what the page is about.
technicalAudit.Fix Instructions: Add an H1 tag to homepage. Suggested H1: 'Online Coding Classes for Kids — Learn Scratch, Python, Minecraft & More'. Only ONE H1 per page.

technicalAudit.Impact: 44% of pages have no meta description. Google may auto-generate one (often poorly).
technicalAudit.Fix Instructions: Write unique meta descriptions for all pages. Priority: top 30 traffic pages first. Keep 150-160 characters. Include target keyword + call-to-action. See 'Meta Tags' tab for specific recommendations.

technicalAudit.Impact: 29% of pages have no H1. Search engines can't properly understand page topic.
technicalAudit.Fix Instructions: Add unique, descriptive H1 tags to all pages. H1 should contain the page's primary target keyword. See 'Meta Tags' tab for specific recommendations.

technicalAudit.Issue: Course catalog pages blocked for crawlers (403 for bots)
technicalAudit.Impact: Course pages work for human visitors but return 403 to Google's crawler. These pages won't appear in search results. Less critical since the main SEO play is pillar pages and blog content, but still worth fixing for long-tail course-specific searches.
technicalAudit.Fix Instructions: Check bot protection settings (Cloudflare, Sucuri, Wordfence, or hosting firewall). Whitelist Googlebot and other legitimate crawlers for /course-catalog/ pages. These pages should be publicly crawlable since they show course descriptions (not the actual paid content). The 'Start Now' button already redirects to /book-consultation/ so there's no security risk in making them crawlable.

technicalAudit.Impact: 58 pages require 7+ clicks from homepage. Google may never crawl them.
technicalAudit.Fix Instructions: Add internal links from higher-level pages (homepage, category pages, pillar pages) to deeply buried content. See 'Internal Linking' tab.

technicalAudit.Impact: Same H1 rendered twice on 119 pages. Likely a WordPress theme template issue.
technicalAudit.Fix Instructions: Edit the WordPress theme template (usually header.php or single.php) to remove the duplicate H1 output. Check if Yoast SEO and the theme are both outputting H1s.

technicalAudit.Impact: No schema markup producing any Google rich results. No star ratings, FAQ dropdowns, course info, or breadcrumbs in search results.
technicalAudit.Fix Instructions: Add JSON-LD structured data. See 'Schema Checklist' tab and the schema template files for ready-to-use code. Priority: Organization (homepage), Course (all course pages), Article (all blog posts), FAQ (key landing pages).

technicalAudit.Impact: 21 pages with very little content. Google may consider them low-quality.
technicalAudit.Fix Instructions: Either expand thin pages to 500+ words with useful content, or noindex them if they're not important (e.g., tag/category archives). Review each page individually.

technicalAudit.Impact: Links to junilearning.com (now redirects to acely.ai) and other moved external sites.
technicalAudit.Fix Instructions: Find and update or remove links to defunct external sites. Check: junilearning.com links should be updated or removed.

technicalAudit.Issue: Temporary redirects that should be permanent
technicalAudit.Fix Instructions: Change 302/307 redirects to 301 if the moves are permanent. Temporary redirects don't pass full link equity.

schemaChecklist.Required Fields: name, url, logo, description, sameAs (social links), contactPoint
schemaChecklist.Implementation Notes: Add to homepage <head>. Include social media URLs, phone, email. See schema-organization.json template.

schemaChecklist.Required Fields: name, description, provider (Organization), courseMode (online), offers (price), educationalLevel
schemaChecklist.Implementation Notes: Add to each course page. Once the 403 issue is fixed, implement course schema on all 120 pages. Use Yoast or custom JSON-LD. See schema-course.json template.

schemaChecklist.Required Fields: headline, author (Person), datePublished, dateModified, image, publisher (Organization)
schemaChecklist.Implementation Notes: Add to every blog post via Yoast SEO or theme template. See schema-article.json template.

schemaChecklist.Apply To: Key landing pages + blog posts with FAQ sections
schemaChecklist.Required Fields: mainEntity: array of Question + acceptedAnswer pairs
schemaChecklist.Implementation Notes: Add to: homepage, /coding-for-kids/, /coding-classes-for-kids/, top blog posts. Enables FAQ rich results (dropdown answers in Google). See schema-faq.json template.

schemaChecklist.Required Fields: itemListElement: array of ListItem with position, name, item (URL)
schemaChecklist.Implementation Notes: Implement via Yoast SEO breadcrumb feature or theme. Shows breadcrumb trail in search results (Home > Blog > Minecraft > Post Title).

schemaChecklist.Apply To: Tutorial blog posts (Minecraft guides, coding tutorials)
schemaChecklist.Required Fields: name, step (array of HowToStep with name, text, image)
schemaChecklist.Implementation Notes: Add to how-to style posts: Minecraft server setup, Roblox game creation, Scratch tutorials. Shows step-by-step cards in Google.

schemaChecklist.Required Fields: reviewRating, author, reviewBody OR ratingValue, ratingCount, bestRating
schemaChecklist.Implementation Notes: Only add if CodaKid has genuine reviews/testimonials. Shows star ratings in search results. Do NOT fabricate reviews.

schemaChecklist.Required Fields: name, description, thumbnailUrl, uploadDate, contentUrl or embedUrl
schemaChecklist.Implementation Notes: Add to pages with YouTube/Vimeo embeds. Shows video thumbnails in search results.
